Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend Stay
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Continued To Aug-01-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EQUIFAX INC.'s Motion To Extend Stay.

The Court invites the California Attorney General's Office to share its views on Equifax's motion to further extend the stay of this action, and requests that the California Attorney General inform the Court, in as much detail as is appropriate under the circumstances, as to the status of the pending multistate investigation and the anticipated timeframe within which it will be completed. The hearing on Equifax's motion to stay is continued to August 1, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, and the existing stay shall be extended until that date.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for full details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Continued To Feb-20-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kristine Poplawski, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415-554-3878, with Kenneth Walczak, Esq., for plaintiff;
Charles Correll, Jr., Esq. of King & Spalding, T:415-318-1209, with Edmund Wang, Esq., for defendant Equifax Inc.

RULING: The Court's tentative ruling was discussed. After hearing from both parties, the Court continues the hearing on DEFENDANT EQUIFAX, INC.'S Motion To Stay to February 20, 2018 at 9:30 am. Counsel for defendant Equifax Inc. is to prepare a brief Order inviting the Attorney General for its view regarding Equifax's motion. The attorney general may file its papers by February 16.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristine Poplawski, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3878 of Office of the City Attorney for Plaintiff.
Charles Correll Jr. 415-318-1209 and Edmund Wang, Esq. 415-318-1206 of King & Spalding for Defendant Equifax Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues CCP 1798.82(a) and 1798.82(d). Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT EQUIFAX INC. DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATES OF CALIFORNIA'S COMPLAINT.

Defendant Equifax Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is taken under submission.* (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Plaintiff submits a proposed order.

Judge: Andrew Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AC)

*Order overruling Defendant Equifax Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is signed after the hearing adopting the Court's tentative ruling* (see scanned order for details)
